banned_breeds.notes: No national breed ban, but certain municipalities may restrict specific breeds.

key_contacts.authority: SAG — Servicio Agricola y Ganadero

Colombia.notes: Colombia has become a growing expat destination, particularly Medellin and Bogota. ICA manages animal import controls.

microchip.notes: Microchip mandatory for dogs and cats.

rabies_vaccination.notes: Annual rabies vaccination required.

rabies_titre_test.notes: Titre test not normally required. Carry documentation in case.

quarantine.notes: No routine quarantine. Veterinary inspection on arrival at El Dorado or other main airports.

import_permit.name: ICA Import Permit
import_permit.how_to_apply: Apply via ICA online portal at least 15 days before travel
import_permit.notes: Import permit required from ICA.

health_certificate.name: Official Veterinary Health Certificate
health_certificate.must_be_endorsed_by: National veterinary authority or USDA/APHA equivalent
health_certificate.notes: Certificate in Spanish or with certified Spanish translation required.

banned_breeds.notes: Some cities restrict specific breeds. No national ban on import of specific breeds, but check Bogota and Medellin municipal rules.

key_contacts.authority: ICA — Instituto Colombiano Agropecuario

Egypt.notes: Egypt has a significant expat community in Cairo. GOVS (General Organisation for Veterinary Services) oversees animal imports. Embassy attestation of health certificate required.
Egypt.official_source: GOVS — General Organisation for Veterinary Services — govs.gov.eg

microchip.notes: Microchip mandatory.

rabies_vaccination.notes: Current rabies vaccination required.

rabies_titre_test.required_for: May be required at customs officer discretion
rabies_titre_test.notes: Carry titre test results. Requirements can vary.

quarantine.notes: No routine quarantine. Inspection on arrival at Cairo International Airport by GOVS vet.

import_permit.name: GOVS Import Permit
import_permit.notes: Import permit from GOVS required in advance.

health_certificate.name: Official Veterinary Health Certificate
health_certificate.attestation: Must be endorsed by national vet authority AND attested by Egyptian embassy
health_certificate.notes: Embassy attestation required. Allow 2 weeks for attestation.

banned_breeds.notes: Pit Bull Terriers banned. Some large breeds may require additional permits.

key_contacts.authority: GOVS — General Organisation for Veterinary Services

Kenya.notes: Nairobi is a major expat hub (UN agencies, NGOs, multinationals). Pet import is permitted with correct documentation. Directorate of Veterinary Services (DVS) is the competent authority.
Kenya.official_source: Directorate of Veterinary Services (DVS), Ministry of Agriculture — kilimo.go.ke

microchip.notes: Microchip required for all imported companion animals.

rabies_vaccination.notes: Current rabies vaccination required.

rabies_titre_test.notes: DVS may request titre results. Carry documentation.

quarantine.notes: No routine quarantine for compliant pets. DVS inspection at Jomo Kenyatta International Airport.

import_permit.name: DVS Import Permit
import_permit.how_to_apply: Apply to DVS at least 21 days before travel
import_permit.notes: Import permit required. DVS reviews documentation.

health_certificate.name: Official Veterinary Health Certificate
health_certificate.issued_by: Official government vet in origin country
health_certificate.notes: Certificate must list vaccinations, microchip, and declare freedom from contagious disease.

banned_breeds.notes: No national breed ban for import, but city-level restrictions may apply. Certain breeds require additional documentation.

key_contacts.authority: DVS — Directorate of Veterinary Services

Nigeria.notes: Pet import possible but requires strict documentation. Federal Department of Veterinary and Pest Control Services (FDVPCS) manages imports. Lagos is the main entry point.
Nigeria.official_source: Federal Department of Veterinary and Pest Control Services (FDVPCS) — fmard.gov.ng

microchip.notes: Microchip required.

rabies_vaccination.notes: Current vaccination required.

rabies_titre_test.notes: Requirements can vary. Carry titre test documentation.

quarantine.notes: Inspection on arrival by FDVPCS. No routine quarantine for compliant pets.

import_permit.name: FDVPCS Import Permit
import_permit.notes: Permit required from FDVPCS. Apply in advance.

health_certificate.name: Official Veterinary Health Certificate
health_certificate.attestation: Must be endorsed by national vet authority

banned_breeds.notes: No specific breed ban nationally, but customs officers may restrict certain breeds.

key_contacts.authority: FDVPCS — Federal Department of Veterinary and Pest Control Services

Czech_Republic.notes: EU member. Czech Republic follows standard EU pet travel rules. Growing expat community in Prague. No routine quarantine.

microchip.timing: Before or on same day as first rabies vaccination

rabies_titre_test.not_required_for: EU/listed countries

banned_breeds.legislation: Czech Act on Municipalities / regional rules
banned_breeds.notes: No national breed ban. Some municipalities may restrict specific breeds in public.

key_contacts.authority: SVA — State Veterinary Administration

Romania.rabies_status: rabies-controlled (some wildlife risk in rural areas)
Romania.notes: EU member. Romania follows standard EU pet travel rules. Large Romanian diaspora in UK/EU creates significant reverse-migration pet movement.
Romania.official_source: National Sanitary Veterinary and Food Safety Authority (ANSVSA) — ansvsa.ro

microchip.timing: Before or on same day as first rabies vaccination

rabies_titre_test.not_required_for: EU/listed countries

banned_types: Romanian Carpathian Shepherd Dog (in urban areas, regulated not banned)
banned_breeds.notes: Pit Bull and crosses are restricted. Rottweiler and other large breeds subject to muzzle/leash rules in public.

key_contacts.authority: ANSVSA — National Sanitary Veterinary and Food Safety Authority

Malta.notes: EU member island nation. Malta is rabies-free and has strict biosecurity. Additional tick treatment required for dogs. No routine quarantine for compliant pets.
Malta.official_source: Ministry for the Environment — msdec.gov.mt / Veterinary Regulation Directorate

microchip.timing: Before or on same day as first rabies vaccination

rabies_titre_test.not_required_for: EU/listed countries

tapeworm_treatment.required_for: Dogs from countries with echinococcosis risk

tick_treatment.drug: Tick prevention product effective against Rhipicephalus ticks
tick_treatment.notes: Malta (and Cyprus) require tick treatment for dogs. Must be recorded in pet passport or AHC.

banned_breeds.legislation: Maltese Dog Ownership and Protection Regulations
banned_breeds.notes: No full breed ban but certain breeds must be muzzled in public and registered as dangerous dogs.

key_contacts.authority: Veterinary Regulation Directorate, MSDEC

Cyprus.notes: EU member island nation. Cyprus has a large British expat community and significant pet transport traffic. EU standard rules plus tick treatment for dogs.
Cyprus.official_source: Department of Veterinary Services, Ministry of Agriculture — moa.gov.cy

microchip.timing: Before or on same day as first rabies vaccination

rabies_titre_test.not_required_for: EU/listed countries

tick_treatment.drug: Tick prevention product effective against Rhipicephalus sanguineus
tick_treatment.notes: Must be documented in pet passport or AHC. Treated by veterinarian.

banned_breeds.notes: Banned breeds cannot be imported.

key_contacts.authority: Department of Veterinary Services, Ministry of Agriculture

Spain.notes: EU member state. Follows EU Regulation 576/2013 (transitioning to Regulation 2026/131 from April 2026). Spain has one of Europe's highest pet ownership rates. Some additional national rules for 'potentially dangerous dogs' (PPP list). Authority: Ministerio de Agricultura, Pesca y Alimentacion (MAPA).

microchip.timing: Must be implanted before or on same day as first rabies vaccination
microchip.notes: EU standard. 15-digit ISO microchip required.

rabies_vaccination.booster_condition: No wait if continuous vaccination record with no lapse
rabies_vaccination.notes: EU-harmonised rules. Vaccination must be recorded in EU pet passport or Animal Health Certificate.

rabies_titre_test.required_for: Pets from non-EU countries not on the EU listed third countries
rabies_titre_test.not_required_for: Pets from EU member states and EU-listed countries
rabies_titre_test.notes: For pets from non-listed third countries: 90-day wait after successful titre test. Blood drawn at least 30 days after rabies vaccination.

quarantine.notes: No routine quarantine for pets entering with correct EU-standard documentation.

import_permit.alternative: EU pet passport or Animal Health Certificate serves as the travel document
import_permit.notes: No separate import permit. The AHC or EU pet passport is the required document.

health_certificate.name: EU Pet Passport (for EU-resident pets) or Animal Health Certificate (AHC) for third-country pets
health_certificate.notes: AHC valid 10 days from issue for entry. Then valid 4 months for onward EU travel.

tapeworm_treatment.required_for: Dogs entering from non-EU countries (check specific country rules)
tapeworm_treatment.notes: Required for dogs from countries where Echinococcus multilocularis is a risk.

banned_breeds.legislation: Real Decreto 1485/1985 and regional laws. PPP (Perros Potencialmente Peligrosos) list.
banned_breeds.notes: PPP dogs require special licence, liability insurance, and muzzle/lead in public. Some regions have stricter rules. Entry may still be possible with documentation but check in advance.

export_permit.notes: No export permit required for cats and dogs.

health_certificate.notes: AHC required for export to non-EU countries. EU pet passport valid for intra-EU movement.

New_Zealand.notes: New Zealand is rabies-free with one of the world's strictest pet import systems. All dogs and cats must complete mandatory quarantine at the government-approved facility (Levin MAF Quarantine Station, now MPI). Import permit required in advance. Only cats and dogs permitted from most countries; birds require separate approval. Authority: Ministry for Primary Industries (MPI), Biosecurity New Zealand.

microchip.timing: Must be implanted before or on same day as first rabies vaccination
microchip.notes: Microchip number recorded on all documentation.

rabies_vaccination.booster_condition: Booster required if lapse in vaccination cover
rabies_vaccination.notes: Vaccination schedule must be complete before titre test blood draw.

rabies_titre_test.required_for: All pets from all countries (no exemptions)
rabies_titre_test.not_required_for: None
rabies_titre_test.notes: 180-day wait (6 months) from successful titre test before pet can enter New Zealand. This wait cannot be shortened under any circumstances. Blood must be drawn at least 30 days after rabies vaccination.

quarantine.facility: MPI Levin Quarantine Facility, Levin, Manawatu (the only approved facility)
quarantine.cost_responsibility: Owner pays all quarantine fees (typically NZD 2,000-4,000+)
quarantine.notes: Mandatory 10-day quarantine for all arriving cats and dogs. Pre-booking required well in advance as capacity is limited. Book before flights.

import_permit.issued_by: Ministry for Primary Industries (MPI), Biosecurity New Zealand
import_permit.notes: Apply for import permit before booking flights. Permit specifies all conditions. Non-compliance results in re-export or destruction.

health_certificate.name: Government veterinary health certificate
health_certificate.issued_by: Government (official) veterinarian in country of export
health_certificate.notes: Must use MPI-specified format for the exact country of export. No standard format accepted. Download the correct template from the MPI website.

banned_breeds.notes: Banned breeds may not enter New Zealand under any circumstances.

export_permit.notes: No export permit for cats and dogs from New Zealand.

health_certificate.notes: MPI issues AHC or equivalent for export.

Oman.notes: Oman is a popular expat destination in the Gulf. Pet imports are regulated by the Ministry of Agriculture, Fisheries and Water Resources (MAFWR). Some breed bans apply. Dogs and cats accepted with prior approval. Authority: MAFWR General Directorate of Veterinary Services.

microchip.timing: Before travel
microchip.notes: Microchip number must match all documentation.

rabies_vaccination.notes: Valid rabies vaccination required. Booster must be within manufacturer's validity period.

rabies_titre_test.required_for: Pets from countries deemed high risk by MAFWR
rabies_titre_test.not_required_for: Some Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) country movements
rabies_titre_test.test_type: FAVN or ELISA at recognised laboratory
rabies_titre_test.notes: Check current requirements with MAFWR as requirements change. UK and Australian pets typically require titre test.

quarantine.notes: No routine quarantine for compliant pets. Veterinary inspection on arrival at Muscat International Airport. Non-compliant pets may face quarantine or re-export.

import_permit.issued_by: MAFWR General Directorate of Veterinary Services
import_permit.notes: Import permit (No Objection Certificate) required before travel. Apply through MAFWR or a local registered agent.

health_certificate.name: Veterinary Health Certificate
health_certificate.issued_by: Official (government) veterinarian in country of export, endorsed by government authority
health_certificate.notes: Certificate must be endorsed by the competent veterinary authority in the exporting country.

banned_breeds.notes: List subject to change. Verify with MAFWR before booking travel.

health_certificate.notes: Health certificate from MAFWR-registered vet for export.

Bahrain.notes: Bahrain is a GCC member with a significant expat population. Pet import regulated by Ministry of Works, Municipal Affairs and Urban Planning - Animal Wealth Directorate. Breed bans apply. Dogs and cats commonly imported by expats.

microchip.timing: Before travel
microchip.notes: ISO microchip required and must match all documents.

rabies_vaccination.notes: Valid rabies vaccination within manufacturer's specified validity.

rabies_titre_test.not_required_for: GCC country movements may be exempt
rabies_titre_test.notes: Check current Bahrain requirements. UK, Australian, and US pets typically require titre test.

quarantine.notes: Veterinary inspection on arrival at Bahrain International Airport. Compliant pets released. Non-compliant pets may face quarantine.

import_permit.issued_by: Animal Wealth Directorate, Ministry of Works, Municipal Affairs and Urban Planning
import_permit.notes: Import permit required before travel. Apply via the Bahrain eGovernment portal or through a local agent.

health_certificate.issued_by: Official veterinarian, endorsed by national authority
health_certificate.notes: Endorsed government health certificate required.

banned_breeds.notes: Banned breeds list may be updated. Verify before travel.

health_certificate.notes: Health certificate from registered vet, endorsed by Animal Wealth Directorate.

Jordan.notes: Jordan regulated by Ministry of Agriculture, General Directorate of Veterinary Services. Expat community in Amman. Process relatively straightforward for cats and dogs from approved countries.

microchip.timing: Before travel

rabies_titre_test.not_required_for: Pets from rabies-free or low-risk countries (UK, Australia, NZ may be exempt)
rabies_titre_test.notes: Verify current requirements with Ministry of Agriculture.

quarantine.notes: Veterinary inspection on arrival. Non-compliant pets may be quarantined.

import_permit.issued_by: Ministry of Agriculture, General Directorate of Veterinary Services

health_certificate.issued_by: Official veterinarian, endorsed by national authority

banned_breeds.notes: Breed restrictions apply. Verify list before travel.

Finland.notes: EU member. Follows EU Regulation 576/2013 and Regulation 2026/131 from April 2026. Finland has specific tapeworm treatment requirements. Authority: Finnish Food Authority (Ruokavirasto).

microchip.timing: Before rabies vaccination

rabies_titre_test.required_for: Pets from non-EU third countries not on EU listed countries list
rabies_titre_test.not_required_for: EU member state pets, EU-listed third country pets

quarantine.notes: No routine quarantine for compliant pets.

health_certificate.name: EU Pet Passport or Animal Health Certificate (AHC)

tapeworm_treatment.notes: Finland has specific Echinococcus risk zone requirements.

banned_breeds.notes: Finland does not have a breed ban list. All breeds accepted subject to welfare rules.

health_certificate.notes: EU pet passport valid for EU movement. AHC for non-EU export.

Hungary.notes: EU member. EU Regulation 576/2013 applies. Authority: National Food Chain Safety Office (NEBIH). Some local breed restrictions.

banned_breeds.notes: Some breeds require special permit and conditions. Verify before travel.

Bulgaria.notes: EU member. EU regulations apply but Bulgaria is not on the UK's Part 1 list (has wild rabies). Pets from Bulgaria to UK require titre test. Authority: Bulgarian Food Safety Agency (BFSA).

rabies_titre_test.required_for: Pets from non-listed third countries (into UK/AU/NZ)

banned_breeds.legislation: Bulgarian Animal Protection Act and municipal orders
banned_breeds.notes: Some municipalities have additional breed restrictions.

Croatia.notes: EU member since 2013. EU Regulation 576/2013 applies. Authority: Ministry of Agriculture, Veterinary and Food Safety Directorate.

banned_breeds.legislation: Animal Protection Act (Zakon o zastiti zivotinja)
banned_breeds.notes: Croatia does not operate a breed ban system.

Morocco.notes: Morocco regulated by the Office National de Securite Sanitaire des produits Alimentaires (ONSSA). Rabies present. Import permit required. Popular expat destination and gateway for Africa.

rabies_titre_test.required_for: Pets from certain countries (check ONSSA list)
rabies_titre_test.notes: Requirements depend on country of origin.

quarantine.notes: Veterinary inspection on arrival. Non-compliant pets may be quarantined or returned.

import_permit.issued_by: ONSSA (Office National de Securite Sanitaire des produits Alimentaires)

health_certificate.issued_by: Official veterinarian, endorsed by national authority

banned_breeds.notes: Breed restrictions apply, verify with ONSSA.

health_certificate.notes: ONSSA-endorsed health certificate.

Pakistan.notes: Pakistan regulated by Ministry of National Food Security and Research. Importing pets to Pakistan is more complex; exporting pets from Pakistan (expats leaving) is a common use case. Authority: Animal Quarantine Department.

rabies_titre_test.notes: Verify current requirements with Animal Quarantine Department.

quarantine.notes: Quarantine inspection required. Duration depends on origin country and documentation status.

import_permit.issued_by: Animal Quarantine Department, Ministry of National Food Security and Research

health_certificate.issued_by: Official veterinarian, endorsed by national authority

banned_breeds.notes: Some breeds face additional restrictions. Verify locally.

health_certificate.notes: Health certificate endorsed by national authority required for export.

Bangladesh.notes: Bangladesh regulated by Department of Livestock Services (DLS), Ministry of Fisheries and Livestock. Large diaspora in UK makes this an active export corridor.

rabies_titre_test.notes: Verify with DLS.

quarantine.notes: Quarantine inspection at entry point.

health_certificate.issued_by: Official veterinarian, endorsed by national authority

banned_breeds.notes: No specific breed ban list; local regulations may apply.

Nepal.notes: Nepal regulated by Department of Livestock Services (DoLS). Popular with expats and hikers. Export corridor to UK/Australia common for expats leaving.

quarantine.notes: Quarantine inspection on arrival.

banned_breeds.notes: No specific breed ban; verify locally.

Cambodia.notes: Cambodia regulated by General Directorate of Animal Health and Production (GDAHP), Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries. Significant expat community (Phnom Penh, Siem Reap). Import and export processes can be complex.

quarantine.notes: Veterinary inspection on arrival. Quarantine if non-compliant.

banned_breeds.notes: No specific list; verify with GDAHP.

Myanmar.notes: Myanmar regulated by Department of Animal Husbandry (DAH), Ministry of Agriculture, Livestock and Irrigation. Complex import/export processes. Expat community in Yangon.

quarantine.notes: Quarantine at designated facility.

health_certificate.issued_by: Official veterinarian, endorsed by national authority

banned_breeds.notes: Verify current breed restrictions with DAH.

Peru.notes: Peru regulated by SENASA (Servicio Nacional de Sanidad Agraria). Growing expat community. Common export corridor to USA, Spain, UK.

rabies_titre_test.required_for: Pets from some countries (check SENASA list)

quarantine.notes: Veterinary inspection on arrival at Lima airport.

banned_breeds.notes: Lima and some regions have breed restrictions for PPP (Perros Potencialmente Peligrosos).

health_certificate.notes: SENASA-endorsed health certificate required for export.

Ecuador.notes: Ecuador regulated by AGROCALIDAD (Agencia de Regulacion y Control Fitosanitario y de Sanidad Animal). Quito and Guayaquil are entry points.

quarantine.notes: Veterinary inspection on arrival.

banned_breeds.notes: Municipal breed restrictions may apply.

Costa_Rica.notes: Costa Rica regulated by SENASA (Servicio Nacional de Salud Animal), part of Ministry of Agriculture and Livestock (MAG). Popular destination for North American and European expats. Relatively straightforward pet import process.

rabies_vaccination.notes: Vaccination must be current within 1 year (or per manufacturer's guidelines).

rabies_titre_test.notes: Costa Rica does not routinely require a titre test. Verify with SENASA for specific origins.

quarantine.notes: No quarantine. Veterinary inspection on arrival. All documents must be in order.

import_permit.notes: Import permit (Permiso de Importacion) required. Apply online via SENASA portal.

banned_breeds.notes: Some municipalities restrict these breeds. Verify locally.

Ghana.notes: Ghana regulated by the Veterinary Services Directorate (VSD), Ministry of Food and Agriculture. Accra is the main entry point. Expat community in Accra/Kumasi. Export corridor to UK common.

rabies_titre_test.notes: Verify with VSD.

quarantine.notes: Government quarantine facility.

health_certificate.issued_by: Official veterinarian, endorsed by national authority

banned_breeds.notes: No official breed ban list; verify locally.

Tanzania.notes: Tanzania regulated by Tanzania Veterinary Laboratory Agency (TVLA) and the Ministry of Livestock and Fisheries. Dar es Salaam and Kilimanjaro are main entry points. Expat community present.

quarantine.notes: Government-approved quarantine facility required.

health_certificate.issued_by: Official veterinarian, endorsed by national authority

banned_breeds.notes: Verify current breed restrictions locally.

Ethiopia.notes: Ethiopia regulated by Ethiopian Institute of Agricultural Research (EIAR) and Ministry of Agriculture Animal and Plant Health Regulatory Directorate. Addis Ababa is the main hub. Ethiopian Airlines hub makes this a transit point for Africa.

quarantine.notes: Quarantine at designated facility.

health_certificate.issued_by: Official veterinarian, endorsed by national authority

banned_breeds.notes: Verify locally.

Zimbabwe.notes: Zimbabwe regulated by the Department of Livestock and Veterinary Services, Ministry of Lands, Agriculture, Fisheries, Water and Rural Development. Harare is the main entry point. Expat and diaspora community active, mainly UK corridor.

quarantine.notes: Government quarantine facility, Harare.

import_permit.issued_by: Department of Livestock and Veterinary Services

health_certificate.issued_by: Official veterinarian, endorsed by national authority

banned_breeds.notes: Verify locally.

export_permit.issued_by: Department of Livestock and Veterinary Services

Mauritius.notes: Mauritius is rabies-free and has extremely strict pet import controls similar to Australia. Pets must have lived in an approved rabies-free country for 6 months before import. Mandatory quarantine applies. Authority: Veterinary Services Division, Ministry of Agro-Industry and Food Security.

rabies_titre_test.notes: 180-day residency requirement in approved country after successful titre test. Only pets from approved countries (similar to Australia Groups) accepted.

quarantine.notes: Mandatory 30-day quarantine for all arriving pets. One of the longest quarantine periods globally.

import_permit.issued_by: Veterinary Services Division, Ministry of Agro-Industry
import_permit.notes: Extremely limited import permits issued. Apply well in advance.

health_certificate.issued_by: Official government veterinarian in approved country

banned_breeds.legislation: Animal Welfare Act and Veterinary Services Division policy
banned_breeds.notes: Banned breeds may not enter Mauritius under any circumstances.

health_certificate.notes: Veterinary Services Division health certificate.

Slovakia.notes: EU member. EU Regulation 576/2013 applies. Authority: State Veterinary and Food Administration (SVFA). No breed ban at national level.

banned_breeds.notes: No national breed ban. Some municipalities may have local restrictions.

Luxembourg.notes: EU member with one of the highest per-capita expat populations in the world (EU institutions, finance sector). EU Regulation 576/2013 applies. Authority: Administration des services veterinaires (ASV).

banned_breeds.legislation: Loi sur la detention des chiens dangereux
banned_breeds.notes: Registered Staffordshire breeds allowed with conditions.

uk_to_australia.complexity: High (quarantine mandatory, titre test, import permit)

key_requirements: May need to transit through approved country (e.g. UK)
Complete all AU import requirements from transit country
uae_to_australia.notes: Most complex route in matrix. Professional agent essential.

key_requirements: Rabies vaccination certificate for dogs 3+ months
CDC Dog Import Form for return

hk_to_uk.notes: High-demand route due to BNO visa scheme. HK rabies-free status simplifies UK entry.

hk_to_australia.origin_classification: HK is AU Group 4 or 5 (verify DAFF classification)
hk_to_australia.notes: Second major BNO-adjacent route. Long lead time is the constraint.

sa_to_uk.notes: Despite SA rabies-endemic status, UK entry is standard listed-country process.

sa_to_australia.notes: Ehrlichia canis testing is SA-specific requirement for Australia entry.

sa_to_usa.notes: Same high-risk process as UAE-to-USA. CDC facility reservation critical.

key_requirements: Enter at designated point of entry
uk_to_france.notes: Post-Brexit change: GB pet passport no longer valid. AHC required. Short Channel crossing route popular.

uk_to_singapore.notes: Simplest Asian destination from UK. Category A status means no quarantine.

sg_to_australia.notes: Group 3 advantage: no 180-day wait. Still requires quarantine.

au_to_singapore.notes: Both countries rabies-free. Simplest bilateral route.

key_requirements: EU Pet Passport with current rabies vaccination and microchip
de_to_fr.notes: Simplest possible route. EU pet passport = free movement. Lowest priority for content.

vaccination_timing_matrix.description: Cross-reference of vaccination timing requirements by destination
vaccination_timing_matrix.usa_entry_low_risk: No wait required (vaccination recommended not required)
vaccination_timing_matrix.australia_entry_group3: 30 days between two vaccinations, then titre test
vaccination_timing_matrix.australia_entry_group4: 30 days between vaccinations, titre test, then 180-day wait from blood draw
vaccination_timing_matrix.canada_entry: No specific wait period stated (must have valid certificate)
vaccination_timing_matrix.singapore_entry_cat_b_c: At least 30 days before export, within 12 months
vaccination_timing_matrix.hong_kong_entry: At least 30 days before import, within 12 months
vaccination_timing_matrix.south_africa_entry: At least 30 days before import, within 12 months

microchip_before_vaccination_rule.notes: Universal best practice: ALWAYS microchip before vaccination regardless of destination. Vaccine may be considered invalid otherwise.

breed_restriction_severity.description: Ranking of breed import restrictions by severity across P1 countries
breed_restriction_severity.most_restrictive: France (Category 1 breeds outright banned from import, Category 2 requires permit + sterilisation + insurance)
breed_restriction_severity.very_restrictive: Germany (4 breeds banned from import at federal level, Bundeslaender add more), Singapore (8+ breeds prohibited including Akita)
breed_restriction_severity.restrictive: UK (4 types banned under Dangerous Dogs Act, type-based assessment)
breed_restriction_severity.moderate: Hong Kong (4 fighting dog types prohibited), UAE (varies by emirate, 8+ breeds restricted)
breed_restriction_severity.least_restrictive: Australia (no federal import breed ban, state-level restrictions), Canada (no federal ban, provincial/municipal BSL), USA (no federal BSL), South Africa (no national import breed ban)
